LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH, SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY, IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE THAT HER ASSERTED INJURIES FROM DEFENDANTS' MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE OCCURRED WITHIN ONE YEAR OF THE FILING OF THIS LAWSUIT AND/OR THAT SHE DID NOT DISCOVER THOSE INJURIES ON A DATE WITHIN ONE YEAR OF THE FILING OF THIS LAWSUIT AND WHAT EVENT OR EVENTS LED TO SUCH DELAYED DISCOVERY. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH, SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with twenty days leave to amend to give plaintiff the opportunity, if she can do so in good faith, to allege that her asserted injuries from defendants' medical negligence occurred within one year of the filing of this lawsuit and/or that she did not discover those injuries on a date within one year of the filing of this lawsuit and what event or events led to such delayed discovery. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
